pr.title: Fixup remaining alignment issues for r40 support
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly-only alignment cleanup for r40 support. The PR added many `.balign 8` directives at the start of data-like sections, especially `.bss`, `.sbss`, `.sdata`, `.sdata2`, `.rodata`, and `.dtors`, and removed or reduced some manual padding such as `.4byte NULL` sentinels and oversized `.skip 0x8` reservations. The author noted it was not yet tested on r40, but that it addressed observed `.bss` and other section alignment issues.
what_changed: Added `.balign 8` after many section declarations so section-local data begins on the expected 8-byte boundary.
Applied the alignment pattern broadly across assembly files in MSL, MetroTRK, Runtime, Dolphin SDK, and Melee game modules.

Adjusted some small-BSS reservations after adding explicit alignment, for example changing `.skip 0x8` to `.skip 0x4` in files such as `asm/MSL/console_io.s`, `asm/dolphin/dsp/dsp.s`, `asm/dolphin/dvd/fstload.s`, `asm/dolphin/gx/GXFifo.s`, `asm/dolphin/jpeg/jpegdec.s`, `asm/dolphin/mcc/fio.s`, and `asm/dolphin/mcc/mcc.s`.

Removed some manual `.4byte NULL` padding entries near section transitions or jump-table/string data and replaced the layout effect with explicit `.balign 8` on the following section, as seen in files such as `asm/MSL/printf.s`, `asm/MSL/string.s`, `asm/dolphin/dvd/dvdfs.s`, `asm/dolphin/dvd/fstload.s`, `asm/dolphin/gx/GX.s`, `asm/dolphin/gx/GXAttr.s`, `asm/dolphin/os/OSError.s`, and `asm/dolphin/pad/Pad.s`.
Touched 186 files with 559 additions and 655 deletions, indicating this was a systematic layout correction rather than a targeted function decompilation.
smart_moves: Used explicit assembler alignment directives instead of relying on blank lines, incidental `.4byte NULL` padding, or oversized `.skip` blocks.
Normalized section starts across many object files, reducing dependence on accidental layout artifacts that could vary under r40 tooling.
Kept changes limited to assembly section/data layout, minimizing semantic risk to executable code paths.
Where explicit `.balign 8` introduced the required padding, removed redundant manual padding so labels retained the intended addresses without accumulating extra bytes.
decomp_lessons: For r40 support, section boundary alignment can matter across many object files, not only in files currently being decompiled.

When moving from implicit or hand-inserted padding to explicit assembler alignment, audit nearby `.skip` sizes and `.4byte NULL` placeholders; otherwise the object may gain extra bytes and shift subsequent symbols.
Prefer `.balign 8` at the beginning of data sections such as `.bss`, `.sbss`, `.sdata`, `.sdata2`, `.rodata`, and `.dtors` when evidence shows the original object requires 8-byte alignment.
Small section layout fixes should be treated as matching-critical: they can affect symbol addresses and object layout even when no instructions change.

The PR body says r40 was not actually tested yet, so the exact r40 validation status is weak evidence; the grounded takeaway is that the patch addressed observed `.bss` and other section alignment issues.
naming_conventions: No new symbol naming convention was introduced; the diff mostly preserves existing generated labels such as `lbl_804D7060`, SDK-style globals such as `__DSP_init_flag`, and named variables such as `StopAtNextInt`.
Section directives use existing assembler section names and attributes, for example `.section .bss, "wa"`, `.section .rodata, "a"`, `.section .sdata`, `.section .sbss`, and `.section .sdata2`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Add `    .balign 8` immediately after section declarations where the following label or data must start aligned.
Replace fake padding such as `    .4byte NULL` at the end of one section with explicit alignment at the beginning of the next section when that better represents the intended object layout.
After adding explicit alignment before a BSS/SBSS label, reduce trailing `.skip` sizes when necessary to avoid double-counting padding.
Use explicit alignment for small data sections as well as BSS; examples include `.sdata`, `.sbss`, `.sdata2`, `.rodata`, and `.dtors`.
Keep alignment-only changes isolated from instruction bodies to make object-layout fixes easier to review and bisect.
review_feedback: No review comments or review feedback were present in the provided PR context.
The only human note in the excerpt is from the PR body: the author had not actually tested on r40 yet, but said the patch fixed observed `.bss` and other section alignment issues.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that tested or reverted r40 alignment support after PR #298.
Search for additional `.4byte NULL` padding near section boundaries that should be represented as `.balign 8`.
Audit assembly files still missing `.balign 8` at data-section starts after this PR.
Compare r40 object output before and after `.bss` and `.sbss` alignment normalization.
